microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 deployment** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining agent configurations, self-service scheduling logic, and integration scripts that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a fully scalable, self-service model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, shipping a rack-level drill program in **4 months** with a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a reusable runbook for continuous validation across enterprise accounts.

Served as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, directing 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility and owning stakeholder relationships from operators to C-suite sponsors.

Built an **internal AI agent** for drill planning and restructured end-to-end workflows, cutting planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — translating directly into faster deployments and measurable throughput gains.
microsoft_2023: Drove **agent adoption** for AI-powered code generation workflows, running customer-facing demos and working sessions across 14 key Azure teams that saved **37 engineering hours monthly** and moved teams from pilot energy to embedded daily use.

Embedded with Azure service teams across 11+ discovery interviews to map workflow gaps, translating business logic into a prioritized roadmap that influenced the product team to ship **intent-based YAML generation** as a core feature.

Improved data retrieval and process quality by implementing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, reducing lookup time by 83% and enabling faster agent configuration across teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by driving cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams — turning a fragmented process into a scalable, repeatable deployment model.

Conducted workflow discovery with 20+ service teams, surfacing **81 hours of manual toil** per region launch and building a roadmap to automate critical paths — a direct analog to redesigning broken flows before deploying agents against them.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track operational toil and automation coverage across 140+ teams, giving leadership the signal needed to prioritize high-impact integration work and close deployment gaps.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and owning end-to-end delivery against a live production environment.

Partnered with IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, aligning cross-functional stakeholders on scope and trade-offs to accelerate the software deployment cycle by 25% across the robotics fleet.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies and owned backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain deployment velocity through a complex, multi-team systems integration.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflow for 200+ monthly proposals — replacing a manual, error-prone process with a structured CRM system that cut handoff failures and accelerated close cycles.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a data-driven go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to surface measurable business outcomes from digital channels.

Identified and closed margin leaks by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, delivering pricing model improvements that gave leadership clear signal on where process changes would drive the most impact.
